data.title: UK's Tatton Asset Management AUM/I rises 11%
data.description:
Overview

  • UK discretionary fund manager's FY26 AUM/I (assets under management/influence) grew 11% yr/yr to £24.2 bln

  • Underlying net inflows totaled £2.8 bln, supported by positive investment performance

  • Board expects FY26 financial results to be towards upper end of market expectations

Outlook

  • Tatton Asset Management targets £30 bln in AUM/I by March 2029

  • Board expects FY26 results towards upper end of market expectations

  • Company notes ongoing geopolitical and economic volatility impacting global markets

Result Drivers

  • NET INFLOWS - Underlying organic net inflows of £2.8 bln supported AUM growth, with a stronger second half contributing to a full-year average of £234 mln per month

  • INVESTMENT PERFORMANCE - Market and investment performance contributed £2.5 bln to AUM/I growth

  • IFA FIRM GROWTH - Number of IFA firms using Tatton's services increased by 108, or 9.7%, to 1,218

Company press release:

Key Details

Metric

Assets under management/influence

Beat/Miss

Actual

GBP 24.2 bln

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the investment management & fund operators peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Tatton Asset Management PLC is GBp876.50, about 35.7% above its April 14 closing price of GBp646.00

  • The stock recently traded at 18 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 19 three months ago

For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact reuters.support@thomsonreuters.com.

data.title: Gold IRA Pros and Cons (2026 Guide Released)
data.description:
Gold IRAs have become increasingly popular among investors looking to protect their retirement savings from inflation and market volatility. While they offer unique advantages, they also come with certain limitations. Understanding both the pros and cons is essential before deciding whether a Gold IRA fits your financial strategy.

NASHVILLE, TN / ACCESS Newswire / April 15, 2026 / IRAEmpire is pleased to announce the release of its latest "Gold IRA Pros and Cons" guide for 2026.

Read the Full Gold IRA Pros and Cons Guide Here

The guide aims to help consumers avoid confusions and learn all the different aspects of gold IRAs including their benefits, risks and more.

IRAEmpire recently ranked the best gold IRA companies of 2026 as well.

Michael Hunt, Senior Writer at IRAEmpire shares, "Whenever you're looking to invest in precious metals, you should do a thorough research to ensure you're well-informed on the product. Our Gold IRA pros and cons guide aims to help consumers with that exact task: researching gold IRA companies."

What Is a Gold IRA?

A Gold IRA is a self-directed retirement account that allows investors to hold physical precious metals such as gold, silver, platinum, and palladium instead of traditional paper assets. These accounts follow the same tax rules as standard IRAs but include additional regulations around storage and eligible assets.

Visit the Best Gold IRA Providers in USA and Consult an Expert

Pros of a Gold IRA

1. Hedge Against Inflation

Gold has historically maintained its value during periods of rising inflation. As the purchasing power of currency declines, gold often retains or increases its worth, making it a strong long-term hedge. This is particularly important for retirees or long-term investors who want to preserve the real value of their savings. Over decades, gold has shown an ability to keep pace with or outperform inflation in uncertain economic environments.

2. Portfolio Diversification

Gold typically has a low correlation with stocks and bonds. Adding it to a retirement portfolio can reduce overall volatility and improve risk management. When equity markets decline, gold often behaves differently, helping balance losses. This diversification effect can lead to a more stable portfolio over time, especially during periods of market stress.

View the Best Free Gold IRA Kits Here

3. Protection During Economic Uncertainty

During market downturns, recessions, or geopolitical instability, gold often performs better than traditional assets. This makes it a "safe-haven" investment. Investors tend to move capital into gold during crises, which can support or increase its value when other assets are falling. This protective characteristic makes it especially appealing during times of global uncertainty.

4. Tangible Asset Ownership

Unlike stocks or digital assets, gold is a physical asset with intrinsic value. This provides a level of security that many investors find reassuring. It is not dependent on the performance of a company or financial institution, which reduces exposure to systemic risks. The idea of owning a real, physical asset adds a psychological layer of confidence for many investors.

5. Tax Advantages

Gold IRAs offer the same tax benefits as traditional or Roth IRAs, including tax-deferred growth or tax-free withdrawals (depending on the account type). This allows investments to compound more efficiently over time. When combined with gold's stability, these tax advantages can make it an effective long-term wealth preservation tool.

Source: https://www.iraempire.com/gold-ira-tax-rules-guide/

6. Long-Term Store of Value

Gold has been used as a store of value for thousands of years. Unlike fiat currencies, which can be affected by monetary policy and inflation, gold's supply is limited. This scarcity supports its long-term value and makes it a reliable asset for preserving wealth across generations.

7. Global Acceptance and Liquidity

Gold is universally recognized and accepted, making it a highly liquid asset. Even within a Gold IRA structure, reputable providers offer buyback programs, allowing investors to convert their holdings into cash when needed. This ensures that while gold is a long-term asset, it still offers flexibility when required.

Cons of a Gold IRA

1. Storage and Custodian Requirements

Gold cannot be stored at home. It must be held in an IRS-approved depository, which adds storage and insurance costs.

2. Higher Fees

Compared to traditional IRAs, Gold IRAs often involve additional costs such as setup fees, annual maintenance fees, and storage charges.

View the Lowest Fees Gold IRAs Here

3. Limited Income Generation

Gold does not produce dividends or interest. Returns are based solely on price appreciation, unlike stocks or bonds that generate ongoing income.

4. Liquidity Considerations

While gold can be sold, the process may not be as quick or seamless as selling stocks or mutual funds. Timing and pricing can also vary.

5. Price Volatility

Although gold is considered stable over the long term, its price can fluctuate in the short term, influenced by market sentiment, interest rates, and global events.

Gold IRA vs Traditional IRA

A traditional IRA focuses on growth through stocks, bonds, and mutual funds, while a Gold IRA emphasizes stability and wealth preservation through physical assets. Many investors choose to combine both to balance growth and risk.

Learn about the Best Gold IRA Company in USA

Gold IRA vs Physical Gold

Investors often compare a Gold IRA with directly owning physical gold to determine which option better fits their financial goals. While both provide exposure to precious metals, they differ significantly in structure, benefits, and use cases.

A Gold IRA is a tax-advantaged retirement account that holds physical gold (and other approved metals) within a regulated framework. The metals are stored in IRS-approved depositories and managed by a custodian. This structure allows investors to benefit from tax-deferred or tax-free growth, depending on whether the account is traditional or Roth. Gold IRAs are primarily designed for long-term retirement planning and wealth preservation.

In contrast, physical gold ownership refers to buying gold coins or bars and holding them personally, either at home or in a private vault. This approach offers direct control and immediate access to the asset. Investors who prefer independence and liquidity often choose this route.

One of the key differences is tax treatment. Gold held within an IRA benefits from retirement account tax advantages, while physical gold held personally may be subject to capital gains tax when sold. Over time, this can impact overall returns.

Another important distinction is storage and security. Gold IRAs require secure, insured storage in approved facilities, which ensures compliance and protection but comes with ongoing fees. Physical gold owners are responsible for their own storage and security, which can involve risks such as theft or loss.

Liquidity and accessibility also vary. Physical gold can be sold or accessed quickly, depending on market conditions. Gold IRAs, while still liquid, involve custodians and processes that may take more time to execute transactions.

However, Gold IRAs offer greater structure and discipline. Because they are tied to retirement accounts, they encourage long-term holding and reduce the temptation to make impulsive decisions based on short-term market movements.

Ultimately, the choice depends on the investor's objectives. A Gold IRA is ideal for those focused on retirement planning, tax efficiency, and structured investing. Physical gold is better suited for investors who want direct ownership, flexibility, and immediate access. Many investors choose to hold both, combining the tax benefits of a Gold IRA with the flexibility of physical gold ownership for a balanced approach.

Who Should Consider a Gold IRA?

A Gold IRA may be suitable for a wide range of investors, particularly those focused on protecting wealth and reducing overall portfolio risk.

Investors Concerned About Inflation

Individuals who are worried about rising inflation and currency devaluation often turn to Gold IRAs as a hedge. Since gold has historically preserved purchasing power over time, it can help protect long-term savings from erosion caused by inflation.

Individuals Seeking Portfolio Diversification

A Gold IRA can be a valuable addition for investors who already have significant exposure to stocks, bonds, or real estate. Because gold often behaves differently from traditional assets, it helps balance risk and reduce overall portfolio volatility, especially during market downturns.

Learn about the Best Free Gold IRA Kits Here

Retirees Looking for Stability

Retirees or those nearing retirement typically prioritize capital preservation over aggressive growth. A Gold IRA can provide stability during uncertain economic periods, helping protect savings that are meant to support long-term financial security.

Those Wanting Exposure to Physical Assets

Some investors prefer tangible assets over purely paper-based investments. A Gold IRA allows individuals to gain exposure to physical precious metals within a regulated and tax-advantaged structure, offering both security and diversification.

Investors Seeking Long-Term Protection

Gold IRAs are best suited for long-term strategies. Investors who are patient and focused on preserving wealth over decades may benefit from holding gold as part of a diversified retirement plan.

However, a Gold IRA may be less suitable for investors focused purely on high growth or passive income. Gold does not generate dividends or interest, and its returns depend primarily on price appreciation. Growth-oriented investors may find equities or other higher-risk assets more aligned with their goals.

Ultimately, a Gold IRA works best as a complementary investment-providing balance, protection, and stability within a broader, well-diversified retirement portfolio.

Key Factors to Consider

Before opening a Gold IRA, it's important to evaluate several key factors to ensure it aligns with your financial goals and expectations.

Total Costs and Fees

Gold IRAs typically involve multiple costs, including account setup fees, annual maintenance fees, storage charges, and dealer markups on metals. Understanding the full cost structure upfront helps avoid surprises and allows you to compare providers effectively.

Reputation of the Provider

Choosing a reputable company is critical. Look for strong customer reviews, transparent business practices, and a proven track record. Established providers are more likely to offer reliable service and clear guidance throughout the process.

Storage and Security Options

Since IRS rules require metals to be stored in approved depositories, evaluate the security measures, insurance coverage, and whether the provider offers segregated or non-segregated storage. These factors directly impact the safety of your investment.

Long-Term Investment Goals

A Gold IRA is best suited for long-term wealth preservation rather than short-term gains. Consider how it fits into your broader retirement strategy and whether it aligns with your risk tolerance.

Allocation Within Your Portfolio

Avoid over-allocating to gold. Most experts suggest using it as a portion of a diversified portfolio to balance risk while maintaining growth potential from other assets.

View the Top Gold IRA Company in USA to Learn More

Final Thoughts

Gold IRAs offer a unique combination of stability, diversification, and protection against economic uncertainty. However, they are not without drawbacks, particularly in terms of costs and income generation.

The best approach is to view a Gold IRA as a complementary investment rather than a replacement for traditional retirement accounts. By understanding the pros and cons, investors can make informed decisions and build a more balanced, resilient retirement portfolio in 2026.

About IRAEmpire

IRAEmpire.com provides independent research, rankings, and educational content on Gold IRAs, retirement planning, and investment strategies. Its mission is to help investors make informed decisions through transparent, data-driven insights.

CONTACT:

Ryan Paulson

ryan@iraempire.com

SOURCE: IRAEmpire LLC

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Yomiuri: Japan's PayPay to Offer Quick Loans to Member Stores by Utilizing Payment Data
data.description:
By Yuma Ikeshita

Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

Major Japanese mobile payment service PayPay Corp. will launch a loan service that allows member stores to borrow up to 10 million yen as soon as the same day.

The service will be launched as early as this month, covering its few million member stores nationwide, which include sole proprietors and companies. Through this service, PayPay will offer loan terms — such as interest rates and maximum loan amounts — to member stores, which can then apply via smartphone.

PayPay will conduct the service together with PayPay Bank Corp., formerly Japan Net Bank Ltd., Japan's first online-only bank. The lender will use daily payment data from member stores to determine loan terms, including maximum loan amounts. Proposed loan terms will be revised to match the improving business performance of a member store.

Applications can be made in as little as one minute via PayPay Corp.'s app for member stores. The service will generally relieve member stores of having to conduct procedures such as document submission.

When banks provide corporate loans, they usually require interviews and the submission of business plans, and it often takes one to three months for borrowers to receive the money. PayPay aims to better respond to the needs of customers by lending money quicker than rival financial institutions through the use of customer payment data.

In March 2024, PayPay launched a service that allows member stores to convert future sales into cash, with a cap of 1 million yen. It also offers a loan service of up to 80 million yen that utilizes the guarantee system of credit guarantee corporations.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-M0000194950-1

data.title: CBOT corn targets $4.49-1/4 to $4.50-1/4 range
data.description:
CBOT corn (CK26) is poised to break a resistance at $4.46-3/4 per bushel and rise into the $4.49-1/4 to $4.50-1/4 range.

The contract has broken a falling trendline. The break suggests a reversal of the downtrend from $4.70-1/2. A projection analysis on the trend reveals a series of resistances and supports.

The nearest support is at $4.44-3/4, a break below which may trigger a fall to $4.42-3/4. On the daily chart, the market has stabilized around a support at $4.42, the 61.8% retracement of the uptrend from $4.20-3/4.

The stabilization was followed by a rise, which looks more like a decent bounce than a pullback toward a rising trendline. A break above $4.48-1/2 may open the way toward $4.55.

** Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. The views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered business, financial, or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial, or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Hilbert Group provides updates on share issues and capital structure
data.description:
Hilbert Group AB (“Hilbert” or the “Company”) provides the following update on certain previously announced share issuances and changes to the Company’s capital structure.

In connection with the previously announced acquisition of Nordark, Hilbert has issued the initial consideration in the form of 2,970,100 newly issued Hilbert B shares to the sellers at a subscription price of SEK 6.80 per share. The subscription price has been determined by the Board after arm’s length negotiations with the sellers and is equivalent to the volume weighted average price during the ten trading days immediately preceding 16 March 2026. Payment has been made by way of set-off, and the deviation from the shareholders' preferential rights was made in order to fulfil the Company's obligations under the relevant share purchase.

Following the previously announced financing arrangement with Helena Global Investment Opportunities 1 Ltd (“Helena Global”), the Company has issued 27,603 convertible notes, at a subscription price of SEK 1,000 per note, for a total amount of SEK 27,603,000, and 368,040 warrants free of consideration, both instruments with the same terms as announced on 5 November 2025, to Helena Global. Additionally, Helena Global has converted previously issued convertibles to 1,628,143 Hilbert B shares at an average conversion price of SEK 5.85 per share.

The Company has also completed certain previously agreed share settlements relating to strategic advisory arrangements, resulting in 358,010 Hilbert B shares being issued at a subscription price of SEK 5.81 per share for a total of app. SEK 2,080,000. The subscription price has been determined based on arm’s length negotiations with the subscribers and it is the Board's assessment that the subscription price is in line with market terms. Payment has been made by way of set-off, and the reason for the deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights is to fulfil the Company’s obligations under a strategic advisory agreement.

For further information, please contact:

Barnali Biswal, CEO or

Niclas Sandström, Co-founder

+46 (0)8 502 353 00

About Us

Hilbert group is a quantitative investment company specializing in algorithmic trading strategies in digital asset markets.

Hilbert Group is a Swedish public company and is committed to providing operational infrastructure, risk management and corporate governance that meets the ever-increasing demands of institutional investors.

Hilbert Group is listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market (ticker HILB B) with Redeye Nordic Growth AB as Certified Adviser.

For more information, visit:www.hilbert.group

Attachments

data.title: Chongqing Changan Automobile May Face Cost Pressures in 2026 — Market Talk
data.description: Chongqing Changan Automobile is likely to face higher cost pressures after 2025 expenses came in above expectations, Citi analyst Jeff Chung says in a note. The bank cuts its 2026-2027 earnings forecasts on the company by 16%-21% to 3.20 billion yuan-3.23 billion yuan. However, Citi expects sales volumes and revenue to increase over the 2026-2027 period. The bank cuts its target price on the stock to 11.100 yuan from 13.500 yuan, lowering its NEV valuation to 1X price-to-sales from 2X previously to align with the sector average. Citi has a neutral rating on the stock. Shares are down 0.4% at 10.07 yuan. (venkat.pr@wsj.com)

data.title: Australian Dollar Rises Against Majors
data.description:
CANBERA (dpa-AFX) - The Australian dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday.

The Australian dollar rose to more than a 1-month high of 113.54 against the yen, a 5-day high of 1.6512 against the euro and a 5-year high of 0.9837 against the Canadian dollar, from yesterday's closing quotes of 113.21, 1.6551 and 0.9814, respectively.

Against the U.S. and the New Zealand dollars, the aussie advanced to 0.7145 and 1.2091 from Tuesday's closing quotes of 0.7129 and 1.2077, respectively.

If the aussie extends its uptrend, it is likely to find resistance around 115.00 against the yen, 1.63 against the euro, 0.99 against the loonie, 0.72 against the greenback and 1.22 against the kiwi.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

data.title: ZAWYA: Emirates Islamic drives SME growth and engagement through AED 1mln business banking campaign
data.description:
Dubai, UAE: Emirates Islamic, one of the leading Islamic financial institutions in the UAE, announced the launch of a new Business Banking campaign, aimed at supporting Small and Medium Enterprises (SME) customers and strengthening business resilience, as part of its continued commitment to businesses across the UAE.

In line with its focus on delivering high-value solutions, the bank has introduced a campaign that rewards customers for strengthening their business banking relationships, while encouraging sustainable growth.

With a total prize pool of AED 1 million, specifically designed to empower and support SMEs in the UAE, the initiative will see over 50 winners during the campaign period. The campaign, which runs from 1 April to 30 June 2026, aims to strengthen Emirates Islamic Business Banking’s relationship with its customers

The initiative rewards customers for growing their average balances in Business Banking Accounts. This approach encourages them to make incremental deposits, while further solidifying Emirates Islamic’s position as a trusted partner in the SME ecosystem.

Currently, over 40,000 SMEs bank with Emirates Islamic’s business banking segment. Businesses can participate in the campaign by increasing and maintaining their average balances in the Account, thereby increasing their chances to win. To further incentivise growth, new-to-bank customers will receive double entries into the draws, providing added benefits for emerging businesses to kickstart their financial journey.

With a total prize pool of AED 1 million, the campaign will reward 55 winners over three months, offering multiple opportunities to win. The grand finale draw will award a top prize of AED 250,000 alongside AED 100,000 prizes for two winners and AED 25,000 prizes for 12 winners. In the preceding months, customers can win AED 10,000 and AED 5,000 prizes.

Mohamed Al Hadi, Head of Retail Banking and Wealth Management at Emirates Islamic said: “At Emirates Islamic, we remain committed to supporting our SME customers as they navigate evolving market conditions. This campaign demonstrates our focus on encouraging sustainable growth while strengthening long-term relationships. SMEs are at the core of our strategic agenda and are integral to the UAE’s economic diversification and growth. This campaign contributes to meaningful, long-term socio-economic progress and enables us to shape a more inclusive and future-ready SME economy that caters to the broader economic momentum of the UAE."

Ghazanfar Naqvi, Head of Business Banking at Emirates Islamic, added: “Emirates Islamic is a customer-first, relationship-driven bank that truly understands and supports the needs of entrepreneurs and businesses. This campaign is designed not only to reward customers but to encourage stronger financial foundations and deeper engagement with the banks. It reflects our commitment to empowering businesses with value-driven, transparent and Shari’ah-compliant offerings. It is a testimony to our ongoing support, rewarding SMEs’ efforts to grow their businesses and helping them achieve their financial aspirations.”

About Emirates Islamic:

Emirates Islamic Bank PJSC (Emirates Islamic) is the Islamic banking arm of the Emirates NBD Group and is a leading Islamic financial institution in the UAE. Established in 2004 as Emirates Islamic Bank, the bank has established itself as a major player in the highly competitive financial services sector in the UAE.

Emirates Islamic offers a comprehensive range of Shariah-compliant products and services across the Personal, Business and Corporate banking spectrum with a network of 39 branches and 231 ATMs/CDMs across the UAE. In the area of online and mobile banking, the bank is an innovator, being the first Islamic bank in the UAE to launch a mobile banking app and offer Apple Pay, as well as being the first Islamic bank in the world to launch Chat Banking services for customers via WhatsApp.

Emirates Islamic has consistently received local and international awards, in recognition of its strong record of performance and innovation in banking. The Bank won the “Islamic Retail Bank of the Year – Middle East” and “Most Innovative Murabaha” awards at the prestigious The Banker’s Islamic Banking Awards 2025.

Additionally, the bank was named ‘Best Islamic Corporate Bank in the World’ and ‘Best Islamic Financial Institution in the UAE’ at the Global Finance - Best Islamic Financial Institutions Awards 2025. Emirates Islamic was also awarded the prestigious title of ‘The World’s Best Islamic Digital Bank’ at the Euromoney Islamic Finance Awards 2025.

As part of its commitment to the UAE community, the Emirates Islamic Charity Fund provides financial aid to those in need, with a focus on food, shelter, health, education and social welfare contributions. For further information please visit www.emiratesislamic.ae

For the latest news regarding Emirates Islamic, please visit www.emiratesislamic.ae/en/news-and-updates

Or please contact:

Amina Al Zarooni

Media Relations Manager, Emirates Islamic

Tel: +971 4 4397430; Mob: +971 56 6405080
